name: Gaye's Sourdough Tips
recipeIngredient: Cup or more of starter (from the fridge)
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove a cup or more of your starter from the refrigerator and bring it to room temperature.
2. Feed the starter with 1 cup of flour and 1 cup of water (or slightly less).
3. Let the starter rise until it becomes bubbly and foamy. The "peak" usually occurs 8 to 10 hours after feeding.
4. Keep the starter warm at a temperature between 75°F and 85°F. If it's cold outside, place it in the oven with the light on.
5. Make your bread during the peak of the starter's activity when it is very active.
6. Return any unused starter to the mother pot.
Variation: You can separate the starter into two batches to create a "beer" starter using flat beer instead of water, resulting in a unique and delicious flavor.
To make your own sourdough recipe, substitute 7/8 cup of starter for 1 cup of liquid and reduce flour by 1/4 to 1/2 cup.
